meaning: adv.不可动摇地,坚定不移地
examples: The mood in Washington was unshakably jolly.
After ten years, a new 300m-set market will exist. This is why I have chosen the solar energy, unshakably.
The novel works on you as unshakably as a bad dream, but such creeping fears descend on the film only with the arrival of Bill Sikes (Jamie Foreman) and Nancy (Leanne Rowe).
